scenarios.title: A degraded ML sidecar surfaces 503 on the opt-in ?strict path

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar is degraded (NATS disconnected) so GET /health computes status:degraded (the live prod condition the redteam observed with HTTP 200)
gherkin.when: an operator / monitoring caller opts in with GET /health?strict=true
gherkin.then: the sidecar returns HTTP 503 with body status:degraded, so a status-code-only consumer sees the degraded state (adversarial: fails against the pre-fix unconditional 200)

scenarios.title: The ?strict opt-in accepts the same truthy variants as the Go contract

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar is degraded (NATS disconnected)
gherkin.when: an operator calls GET /health?strict=<v> for v in {1, true, TRUE, Yes, yes}
gherkin.then: every case-insensitive truthy variant yields HTTP 503, matching the Go healthStrictRequested contract (adversarial: fails against the pre-fix unconditional 200)

scenarios.title: The DEFAULT /health stays 200 when degraded (container-liveness non-destabilization)

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar is degraded (NATS disconnected)
gherkin.when: the Docker HEALTHCHECK sends the DEFAULT GET /health (no ?strict param)
gherkin.then: the sidecar returns HTTP 200 with body status:degraded, so a degraded-but-alive sidecar is NOT marked unhealthy / restart-flapped (non-destabilization invariant: fails if a naive fix flips the default to 503)

scenarios.title: The ?strict opt-in stays 200 when the sidecar is up (no false alarms)

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar is up (NATS connected)
gherkin.then: the sidecar returns HTTP 200 with body status:up, so the opt-in readiness path does not raise a false alarm on a healthy sidecar
